acceptedQuestions.subject: Science
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) mission:
1. It is the world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band radars on a single platform.
2. Under this joint mission, NASA provided the S-band radar while ISRO contributed the L-band radar.
3. It marks the first instance of ISRO using a Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV) to place a satellite into a Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO).
How many of the above statements are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation:
Statement 1 is correct: NISAR is the world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) on a single platform.
Statement 2 is incorrect: The contributions are reversed. NASA provided the L-band radar, while ISRO contributed the S-band radar.
Statement 3 is correct: The mission marks the first time a GSLV rocket (GSLV-F16) was used to place a satellite into a Sun-Synchronous Polar Orbit (SSO).
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: NISAR integrates L-band (NASA) and S-band (ISRO) SAR on a single platform and was uniquely launched into an SSO using a GSLV rocket.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: It is world's first Earth observation satellite to integrate both L-band and S-band Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) on a single platform. NASA provided the L-band radar... ISRO contributed the S-band radar... Launch Vehicle: ISRO’s Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle (GSLV)-F16. Orbit: Sun Synchronous Polar Orbit. It is the first time a GSLV rocket was used to place a satellite into a Sun-Synchronous orbit (SSO).
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Good factual coverage of the mission's unique aspects. Statement 2 uses a classic UPSC swap.

reviewerNotes: Selected three distinct questions covering the launch/agency details, technical imaging features, and mission applications. Rewrote the technical question to test the difference between microwave and optical imaging, which is a highly relevant concept for UPSC.
